title: I2C open-drain signaling and pull-up sizing
description: I2C lines are open-drain so both SDA and SCL need external pull-ups sized for the bus capacitance and target speed.
body: Use when an I2C bus has slow edges or fails at higher speeds. Do not assume internal pull-ups suffice, since they are usually too weak for real bus capacitance. Actions: estimate total bus capacitance, size the pull-up resistors for the rise-time target, and inspect the edges on a capture. Validation signals include rise times within the spec for the chosen speed and clean logic levels at every node. Risk boundary: pull-ups too weak cause sporadic data errors that look like software bugs, so flag slow edges as a physical-layer cause before chasing firmware.
